message:
feat(fts,indexer): consolidate FTS rebuild into rusqlite, detect incomplete historical salvage, and add schema v13 fast-forward

Four interconnected improvements to FTS management and historical
database salvage:

## FTS rebuild consolidation (storage/sqlite.rs, lib.rs)

Replace the frankensqlite-based FTS rebuild path (rebuild_fts_on_connection,
register_fts5_on_connection) with a single rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite()
function that uses rusqlite directly. The new function:
- Clears ALL fts_messages* rows from sqlite_master via writable_schema
  (handles corrupt/orphaned FTS shadow tables, not just DROP IF EXISTS)
- Checkpoints WAL before reopening to ensure a clean state
- Reopens the connection so the fresh schema is visible without stale
  cache
- Wraps INSERT INTO fts_messages in a transaction for atomicity
- Returns the inserted row count for logging

The doctor/recreate_fts_table_via_rusqlite path now delegates to this
same function, eliminating duplicated FTS logic.

## Post-full-rebuild FTS trigger (indexer/mod.rs)

After a full index run (--full), automatically rebuild the stock-
compatible FTS table. This ensures that the FTS index reflects the
rebuilt canonical state, which is critical when the full rebuild
re-imports historical data that wasn't previously FTS-indexed.

## Incomplete historical salvage restart (indexer/mod.rs)

New full_rebuild_requires_historical_restart() detects when a previous
full rebuild was interrupted mid-way through historical bundle salvage:
- Checks for in-progress bundle markers (historical_bundle_progress:*)
- If no progress markers but bundle count > 0 and canonical DB is
  populated: checks whether all bundles were already salvaged, schema
  is at v14, and fts_messages exists — if any condition fails, triggers
  a full restart from a fresh canonical database

This prevents partial salvage state from persisting across interrupted
--full rebuilds, which previously left databases with missing historical
data that could only be fixed by manually deleting the DB.

## Schema v13→v14 fast-forward (storage/sqlite.rs)

New fast_forward_schema_v13_fts_via_rusqlite() detects databases stuck
at schema version 13 (which lacked the FTS table) and rebuilds FTS +
bumps to v14 via rusqlite, without running the full frankensqlite
migration pipeline. Also records the migration in _schema_migrations
if that table exists.

## Historical seed reopening fix (indexer/mod.rs)

After seeding an empty canonical database from a historical bundle, the
reopened connection now uses open_writer() (skipping migrations) instead
of open() (which reruns migrations). This prevents the migration system
from corrupting the freshly-seeded schema by re-applying migrations
that were already applied in the source bundle.

When a historical seed was used, skip the incremental salvage pass
since the seed already contains the full bundle content.

Cargo.lock updated for memchr dependency in fsqlite-types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
